summary: A reproducible Sourcey 3.6.3 build documents twenty real GitHub Gists REST operations from GitHub's maintained MIT-licensed OpenAPI repository at an immutable commit, with split pages, search, portable context files, and a governed runx validation path.

pages.description: Lightweight redirect to the generated reference

observations.detail: Source repo: https://github.com/github/rest-api-description; exact commit 1847198856aa730d19ac7fef6f2617ecbec8e01e and blob 26ab99a5c0ffe590185a3dfed7c96bf5871a7996.
The upstream repository license is MIT, verified through GitHub's repository license endpoint and LICENSE.md.
Sourcey 3.6.3 OpenAPI adapter reads the pinned api.github.com YAML description; no hand-written endpoint placeholders are used.
Reproduction command: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile && pnpm run build; configuration is sourcey.config.ts and split-openapi.mjs prunes referenced components.
Generated navigation contains gists.html and gist-management.html, ten genuine REST operations each, plus llms.txt and a 41-entry search index.
Twenty Gists operations are documented from the source spec, including listing, creating, retrieving, updating, deleting, starring, un-starring, forking, and revision access.
Governed validation uses exact output: runx-cli 0.6.8, satisfying the bounty minimum of runx-cli 0.6.6.

The site intentionally documents the coherent Gists surface rather than all 1,000+ GitHub REST operations; Sourcey renders source descriptions and schemas but does not create upstream line-number links.

Sourcey 3.6.3 omitted consola from its package dependencies and required an explicit compatible dependency; a large single page also needed deterministic splitting and minification for reliable static delivery.

verification.observed_title: GitHub REST API — Gists Reference - API Reference
